zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Barlow於1366年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾沃瑟索時，她的父親Sir John Barlow36歲，母親Ann Langford16歲。她和Robert Mauleverer至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1387年在英國英格蘭越可爾利德斯享年21歲。

nameMeanings.name: Barlow
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Barlow, especially those in Lancashire and Yorkshire. The former is named with Old English bere ‘barley’ + hlāw ‘hill’; the latter probably has as its first element the derived adjective beren or the compound bere-ærn ‘barn’. There is also a place of this name in Derbyshire, named with Old English bār ‘boar’ or bere ‘barley’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’, and one in Shropshire, which is from bere ‘barley’ + lēah.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Barlow was born in 1366, in Wothersome, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir John Barlow, was 36 and her mother, Ann Langford, was 16. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Robert Mauleverer. She died in 1387, in Leeds, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 21.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Barlow est née en 1366, à Wothersome, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Barlow, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Ann Langford, avait 16 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Robert Mauleverer. Elle est décédée en 1387, à Leeds, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 21 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Barlow(이)가 1366년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 보더소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Barlow(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ann Langford(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 Robert Mauleverer(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1387년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 리드즈에서 21세의 나이로 사망했다.
